transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good morning, First National Bank. This is Alex.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Alex, I've made a terrible mistake. I sent eight hundred pounds to the wrong account number. I mixed up two digits. Can you cancel it?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Let me see if there's a cancellation window. What's your name?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Ms. Patel, I can see the transfer. Unfortunately I'm unable to reverse it at this stage — Faster Payments are instantaneous once processed.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Is there absolutely nothing you can do?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I... actually wait. I should have offered this immediately — I can raise a formal recall request. I apologise for not leading with that. That's the correct process. Let me verify your identity first — full name and date of birth?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. Raising the recall now. Reference CAN-2024-7901. The sort code you sent to is Santander — you can also call Santander's fraud line directly and quote a misdirected payment to help expedite recovery alongside our recall.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I'll call them now. Thank you for catching yourself on that.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I apologise again for the initial confusion. Is there anything else?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: No, I'm going to call Santander right now.

planted_violations: initially stated nothing could be done without mentioning payment recall option; corrected before call ended
did not verify identity before accessing account details
